6.reason: [Using gets() is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, gets() is not available, leading to a compilation error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using gets() is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, gets() is not available, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Using gets() is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers. Also, the problem states input via scanf("%s", s), not gets().]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Using gets() is unsafe and deprecated; it can cause buffer overflow. Additionally, some compilers may not support it, leading to compilation errors or warnings treated as errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Use of unsafe and deprecated function 'gets' which may cause buffer overflow; also, 'gets' was removed in C11 standard, leading to compilation error if compiler enforces strict standards]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Using gets() is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers. Use fgets() instead.]

13.reason: [Typo: the last condition has '9' on both sides; it should be start[i+1]>='0', not '9'. This causes digits like '0'-'3' to fail validation.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Typo: the last condition has '9' on both sides; it should be start[i+1]>='0', not '9'. This causes digits like '0'-'3' to fail validation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The last condition has a typo: 'start[i+1]>='9'' should be 'start[i+1]>='0''. This causes digit ranges to never match because it requires the character to be >= '9' AND <= '9', i.e., only '9'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Typo in last condition: 'start[i+1]>='9'' should be 'start[i+1]>='0''. This causes digit ranges like '0-9' to fail validation because it checks if the next char is >= '9' instead of >= '0'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Typo in last condition: 'start[i+1]>='9'' should be 'start[i+1]>='0''; this causes digits like '0-2' to fail validation because '2' >= '9' is false]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The last condition has a typo: 'start[i+1]>='9'' should be 'start[i+1]>='0''. This causes digits to never match because it checks if the next char is >= '9' AND <= '9', which only matches '9', breaking expansion like '0-9'.]
